setup.unlockDesc: Unlock your SSH vault to continue
setup.createDesc: Your master password secures all your SSH credentials, It never leaves this browser
setup.briefDesc: Your server credentials are encrypted in the browser using an AES-256-GCM key derived from your master password. Only ciphertext is stored on the server — the backend never sees your plaintext credentials. SSH connections, file management, and system monitoring all run in-browser via Go WASM, with the backend acting only as a transparent TCP proxy. One master password manages everything.
setup.networkErrorDesc: Unable to connect to the backend server, Please check your network or server status
setup.kdfDesc: Choose the Key Derivation Function used to protect your vault
setup.connFailed: Failed to connect to backend server
setup.secBrowserItem1: Master password resides only in memory, never transmitted
setup.secBrowserItem2: Target SSH credentials are encrypted locally
setup.secBrowserItem3: Go WASM runs full SSH/SFTP client and system monitor
setup.secBackendItem3: Transparent TCP proxy: forwards raw bytes without protocol inspection
setup.secFlow1Desc: Only transmits AES-GCM ciphertext, backend cannot read credentials
setup.secFlow2Desc: Raw TCP byte stream tunneled over WebSocket; SSH encryption makes traffic opaque to backend
setup.secFlow3Desc: Transparent TCP relay to target; backend has zero SSH protocol awareness
setup.secProtectYesDesc:
Even if the backend database is fully compromised, attackers cannot decrypt your SSH credentials without your master password.
SSH session content is protected by the SSH protocol's own end-to-end encryption between the browser WASM client and the target server.
The backend transparent proxy sees only opaque encrypted bytes — it cannot inspect session content.
setup.secProtectNoDesc:
The backend transparent proxy can observe connection metadata (target IPs, ports, timing, traffic volume) but cannot decrypt SSH session content or access credentials.
However, if the backend host is compromised, an attacker could:
(1) Monitor your connection patterns
(2) Tamper with the WASM binary served to the browser
Deploy the backend in a trusted environment, use HTTPS, and verify SSH host keys when prompted.

serverList.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this server?

security.title: Security & Encryption
security.warning: Changing these settings will decrypt and re-encrypt all your vault data, All other devices will be logged out

serverForm.fillRequired: Please fill in all required fields
serverForm.hostKeyHelp: If cleared, the system will prompt you to verify and trust the host identity again on the next connection

terminal.hostKeyPromptMismatchTitle: WARNING: REMOTE HOST IDENTIFICATION HAS CHANGED!
terminal.hostKeyPromptNew:
The authenticity of host can't be established.
SHA256 key fingerprint is:
{fingerprint}
Are you sure you want to continue connecting?
terminal.hostKeyPromptMismatch:
Possible MITM Attack!
It is possible that someone is doing something nasty! Someone could be eavesdropping on you right now (man-in-the-middle attack)! It is also possible that a host key has just been changed.
New SHA256 key fingerprint is:
{fingerprint}
Are you sure you want to overwrite the old key and continue connecting?
terminal.hostKeyAccepted: New host key accepted and saved to vault, Reconnecting..
terminal.commandPlaceholder: Type command here... Press Ctrl+Enter to send

settings.title: Global Settings
settings.keywordHighlightDesc: Automatically highlight keywords in terminal output
settings.autoOpenMonitorDesc: Automatically popup system resource monitor when server state is ready
settings.encryptHandshakeDesc: Use ECDH + AES-256-GCM to encrypt the WebSocket handshake that carries connection metadata (JWT token, target host, port). Tunnel data is already protected by SSH encryption and unaffected by this setting.

argon2id.label: Argon2id (Recommended)
argon2id.desc: 2015 Password Hashing Competition winner, Memory-hard hash that resists GPU/ASIC brute force by consuming large amounts of RAM, Currently the most secure KDF, Downside: requires WASM, slightly slower first unlock

pbkdf2.label: PBKDF2-SHA512
pbkdf2.desc: Classic standard algorithm, Native browser Web Crypto API support, no WASM needed, best compatibility and fast unlock, Downside: relies only on iteration count, no memory cost, weaker against GPU attacks than Argon2id

fileManager.name: Name
fileManager.tooLarge: File is larger than 500KB, Please download it to view
fileManager.fileChangedOnServer: The file has been modified on the server, Force saving will overwrite remote changes, Continue?
